title: Thru the Fire
subtitle: Survival · rebuilding · purpose
description: A project about surviving loss, rebuilding with intention, and deciding what matters most.
story: Thru the Fire is a creative record of loss, survival, and rebuilding. The project asks a difficult question without pretending there is an easy answer: when time collapses, what do you protect, and what do you learn to release?
credits: Music, concept, and visual direction — 2Fly Keith Logan
Interactive experience — What Would You Save?
Archive format — music, film, gameplay, and rebuilding notes

notes.label: PROJECT NOTE
notes.title: A record of rebuilding
notes.text: The song, film, and interactive experience are designed as one connected resilience universe.

notes.label: EXPERIENCE NOTE
notes.title: Choices under pressure
notes.text: The playable experience turns the project theme into a timed reflection on memory, value, and survival.

notes.label: ARCHIVE NOTE
notes.title: Built to expand
notes.text: Additional photographs, press, production credits, and rebuilding updates can remain attached to this permanent page.

title: Streams
subtitle: Value · attention · legacy
description: A project about the difference between attention, value, ownership, and what remains after the current passes.
story: Streams explores the difference between attention and value. Digital media, reactions, money, ownership, and legacy move through the same current—but they do not carry the same weight.
credits: Design language — water, media objects, value, and attention

notes.label: PROJECT NOTE
notes.title: Value versus attention
notes.text: The project asks what remains after the current passes and the reaction count disappears.

notes.label: GAME NOTE
notes.title: Move upstream
notes.text: The player climbs against the current using digital-media objects while collecting value and resisting attention.

notes.label: ARCHIVE NOTE
notes.title: A growing media ecosystem
notes.text: Future releases, alternate visuals, lyrics, production notes, and gameplay updates can live here without losing their context.

title: I Woke Up in Africa
subtitle: Awakening · identity · purpose
description: A project centered on awakening, heritage, service, reflection, and purpose.
story: I Woke Up in Africa documents an awakening shaped by Rwanda, service, heritage, landscape, community, and perspective. The central question remains: did I wake up in Africa, or did Africa wake me up?
credits: Creator, narrator, and producer — 2Fly Keith Logan
Documentary and soundtrack concept — I Woke Up in Africa
Filmed during the Rwanda service journey
Archive format — documentary chapters, music, reflection, and mission

notes.label: DOCUMENTARY NOTE
notes.title: The journey continues
notes.text: The archive is designed to hold the documentary in chapters rather than reducing the experience to a single trailer.

notes.label: MISSION NOTE
notes.title: Service beyond the film
notes.text: The project connects creative storytelling with the continuing service mission and future work in Rwanda.

notes.label: ARCHIVE NOTE
notes.title: Two documentary chapters included
notes.text: Part I and Part II are presented together here so visitors can remain inside the same visual world.

title: I Was Away
subtitle: Reflection · distance · return
description: A reflective project about distance, return, maturity, and perspective.
story: I Was Away is a reflective return—distance turned into perspective, absence turned into maturity, and the quiet recognition that coming back does not mean returning unchanged.
credits: Music and visual concept — 2Fly Keith Logan
Project language — distance, reflection, return, and perspective
Archive format — song, visual story, journal notes, and imagery

notes.label: PROJECT NOTE
notes.title: Distance changes the view
notes.text: The project treats time away as a lens rather than an empty space.

notes.label: VISUAL NOTE
notes.title: Return without pretending
notes.text: The visual world remains restrained, introspective, and intentionally less performative.

notes.label: ARCHIVE NOTE
notes.title: A permanent journal page
notes.text: Future lyrics, photographs, essays, and alternate versions can be attached to this project without rebuilding the site.
